scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Pilots largely work with cognitive and digital tasks: flight planning, navigation, system monitoring, communication, and ongoing decision-making. These aspects have clear potential exposure to AI through decision support, automation of monitoring, route optimization, and risk analysis. At the same time, the role is strongly linked to physical reality, safety-critical real-time assessments, responsibility during operations, and collaboration with crew, air traffic control, and passengers. Additionally, certification, regulations, and Swedish labor market conditions limit how much AI can replace core tasks. Overall, this is a distinctly exposed but not fully digitalizable professional role, which justifies a medium-high score.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For pilots, there are observable AI signals in 2026, but they are still mostly operational support rather than mainstream decision-making. Airlines and aircraft operators are using AI/ML in adjacent functions such as predictive maintenance, fuel optimization, disruption management, scheduling, and some cockpit-adjacent decision support. In Sweden and internationally, pilot-facing AI tools exist for briefing, weather/risk analysis, checklist assistance, and training/simulation, but they are not broadly integrated into the core act of flying or replacing pilot judgment. Safety certification, regulation, and liability constraints keep adoption cautious. So this fits early adoption: tools exist and some employers are experimenting, but it is not yet a widely deployed, daily AI-heavy occupation.

description: Genomför teoretisk och praktisk körkortsutbildning för motorfordon. Lär ut trafikregler, körteknik och trafiksäkerhet.
descriptionEnglish: Conducts theoretical and practical driving lessons for motor vehicles. Teaches traffic regulations, driving techniques, and road safety.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The traffic instructor has a mixed task profile. A substantial part of the job is cognitive and screen-based: explaining traffic rules, planning lessons, assessing student progress, and handling theory instruction could be supported by AI. However, the core occupation also depends on real-time in-car teaching, physical presence, situational judgment in traffic, interpersonal coaching, and safety supervision, which AI cannot replace. In Sweden, practical driver training is also constrained by regulation and professional practice, limiting delegation. Overall, this is moderate exposure rather than high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption signals for driving instructors are marginal to early. There are some observable tools that can support lesson planning, test-question generation, administrative scheduling, and basic learner communication, but there is little evidence of AI being actively embedded in the core occupational task: hands-on driving instruction and real-time safety coaching. In Swedish job-market signals, this occupation does not commonly show AI-specific requirements or widespread tooling mentions, and it is not comparable to occupations where AI is already a routine part of daily work. Any current use appears to be isolated experimentation by individual schools or driving schools rather than mainstream deployment.

description: Samordnar, leder, planerar och fördelar arbetet inom kontor och kundservice. Utbildar och instruerar personal.
descriptionEnglish: Coordinates, leads, plans, and allocates work within offices and customer service. Trains and instructs staff.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This role is primarily administrative and knowledge-intensive: coordination, planning, allocation of tasks, instructions, training, and some customer service management. Much of the core tasks are carried out in digital systems and consist of text, scheduling, case management, follow-up, and decision support, which makes them relatively exposed to AI. At the same time, there is a clear human component in leadership, staff support, conflict resolution, and practical situational adaptation that AI cannot fully replace. Overall, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are clear and observable AI signals for this type of role in Sweden by early 2026, but they are not fully mature or completely standardized. Team leaders for office staff work closely in areas where AI is already practically used: scheduling, internal communication, customer service, meeting notes, case prioritization, standardized responses, and support for staff. In Swedish workplaces, this is reflected in the increasing use of generative AI tools for text production, summarization, translation, and administrative support, especially in the private sector and larger organizations. At the same time, the role is often more operational and organizational than technology-intensive, meaning that adoption primarily happens as productivity support rather than as a core tool. In the public sector, where many such roles also exist, the implementation is slower due to governance, information security, and procurement requirements. Overall, this suggests significant but not universal adoption, around 6.5.

description: Utför annat förmedlingsarbete än inom 3331 – 3335. Exempel på arbetsuppgifter: Säljer varor eller fast egendom på auktion. Bokar engagemang och förhandlar kontrakt m.m. för artister, musiker, författare, idrottsutövare m.fl. Säljer annonser i media. Ger råd om butiksekonomi och marknadsföring.
descriptionEnglish: Carries out other mediation work outside of categories 3331-3335. Examples of tasks include: Selling goods or real estate at auction, booking engagements and negotiating contracts for artists, musicians, authors, athletes, etc. Sells advertisements in media. Advises on retail economics and marketing.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation consists largely of knowledge and office work: selling advertisements, negotiating contracts, booking assignments/engagements, providing advice on retail economics and marketing, as well as managing auctions. Much of this can be supported by AI through text generation, matching, research, pricing, customer communication, and contract support. At the same time, the role often requires negotiation, relationships, local knowledge, judgment, and certain personal business contacts that AI cannot fully replace. Overall, the exposure is clearly high but not nearly entirely digital, therefore around 7.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: The intermediary role in SSYK 3339 is broad and includes several sub-tasks where AI is already evident in practice, but not as a standardized core tool across the profession. Clear adoption signals exist for advertising sales, marketing advice, contract documentation, and some matching/planning through generative AI tools, CRM, and sales support. At the same time, the intermediation work itself is often relationship-driven, negotiation-intensive, and reliant on networks, which means that full-scale AI integration is still uneven. In Swedish job advertisements for related sales, media, and marketing roles, there are increasing demands for AI proficiency, but this indicates more early growth than widespread, mature adoption. Overall: clear but not dominant current signals, thus early adoption with some dispersion.

description: Matchar lediga platser mot arbetssökande. Fångar upp arbetsmarknadens rekryteringsbehov. Ger råd och stöd till arbetsgivare hur de rekryterar personal och till arbetssökande hur de får arbete.
descriptionEnglish: Matches job vacancies with job seekers. Identifies recruitment needs within the labour market. Provides advice and support to employers on how to recruit staff and to job seekers on how to secure employment.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Employment advisors primarily work with information gathering, matchmaking, counseling, and decision-making/support based on labor market data, CVs, requirement profiles, and documentation. Most of this is digital and knowledge-based, making the core tasks relatively exposed to AI: AI can assist with candidate-job matching, text processing, recommendations, and administrative handling. At the same time, the role requires a lot of human interaction, motivational interviewing, local knowledge, and assessment in a Swedish administrative and labor market context, which limits full automation. Overall, the exposure is high but not total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Employment agents work with matching, counseling, and case management support, which includes several clearly AI-relevant components. However, as of early 2026, there are not strong observable signals that AI is broadly and routinely integrated into the professional role in Sweden. There is likely some use of generative tools to formulate job ads, summarize candidate information, suggest matches, and manage simpler communication, particularly in larger or more digitized organizations. At the same time, the Public Employment Service and similar public labor market actors are often more cautious about widespread AI deployment due to regulations, confidentiality, equal treatment, and procurement requirements. Overall, this appears to be clear early adoption with occasional/limited organizational use, but not yet an established, fully normalized AI-supported professional practice.

description:
Producerar och organiserar turist-, affärs-, grupp- och konferensresor. Arrangerar och samordnar konferenser, evenemang, tillställningar, fester och seminarier. Undersöker resmål. Förhandlar om pris och andra villkor samt skriver avtal. Sammanställer resekataloger, tidtabeller och broschyrer. 

Hit hör inte
Resebyråchef (1790)
Resesäljare/Reseförsäljare/Resekonsulent (4221)
descriptionEnglish: Produces and organizes tourist, business, group, and conference trips. Arranges and coordinates conferences, events, receptions, parties, and seminars. Researches travel destinations. Negotiates prices and terms and writes contracts. Compiles travel catalogs, timetables, and brochures.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is largely knowledge- and computer-based: it involves planning and organizing trips, conferences, and events, researching destinations, negotiating terms, writing contracts, and compiling catalogs and brochures. Many of these tasks can be strongly supported or partially automated by AI, especially information retrieval, drafting texts, comparing options, and scheduling. At the same time, the role requires significant human coordination, customer contact, negotiation, adaptation to unforeseen situations, and practical coordination with multiple stakeholders, which means it is not completely AI-driven. Overall, this results in a high but not maximal exposure to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3332 (event and travel producers/coordinators), there are clear current AI adoption signals, but they look more like early-to-mid adoption than mainstream, role-wide transformation. Observable use cases today include AI for drafting itineraries, travel brochures, event copy, agenda/schedule proposals, supplier comparison, translation, and summarizing venue/resort research. Many employers can now access these tools through common office suites and travel/event software, and Swedish workplaces are increasingly mentioning AI in job ads. However, the core job still depends heavily on human negotiation, relationship management, logistics coordination, and handling bespoke client requirements—areas where AI is assisting rather than replacing or deeply embedding into workflows. In Sweden, adoption in this type of coordination/creative-commercial role appears uneven across employers: larger travel companies and event agencies are more likely to use AI tools, while smaller firms and public/association-organized events tend to adopt more cautiously. Overall: real current use, but not yet ubiquitous.

description:
Organiserar för kunders räkning godstransporter och förhandlar om fraktavtal. Upprättar handlingar samt tecknar försäkringar. Beräknar utförselavgifter, tull- och införselavgifter, skatter m.m. Förmedlar lastutrymme i fartyg och andra transportmedel. Utreder reklamations- och skadeärenden. 

Hit hör inte
Logistiker (2141)
Speditionsassistent (4323)
Transportledare (4323)
descriptionEnglish: Organizes freight transport on behalf of clients and negotiates shipping agreements. Prepares documentation and arranges insurance. Calculates export duties, customs and import fees, taxes, etc. Provides freight space on ships and other transport means. Investigates claims and damages.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for freight forwarders and transportation brokers in the coming year. This implies that individuals with the necessary education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but depending on the focus within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In the two-year outlook, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for freight forwarders and transportation brokers will be small to medium. Opportunities in the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 5300 yrkesverksamma speditörer och transportmäklare i Sverige, varav cirka 49 procent är kvinnor och 51 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Freight forwarders and transport intermediaries primarily work with planning and managing freight transport, negotiating shipping contracts, preparing documents, calculating duties/charges, procuring insurance, and handling claims. It is essentially a digital, regulation-driven, and knowledge-intensive job where AI can support or automate much of the information retrieval, document preparation, pricing and fee calculation, as well as some customer communication. However, the role often requires negotiation, responsibility, exception handling, and contact with customers, carriers, and authorities, making it not fully automatable. Overall, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Freight forwarders and transport brokers work in a highly administrative, rule-driven, and document-intensive environment where AI already has clear, observable applications: email and customer responses, quotes/freight contract drafts, document control, customs and fee calculations, case management, and damage/complaint classification. In Swedish and Nordic logistics, this is primarily seen as generative assistants and workflow automation in TMS/ERP, rather than full autonomy. There are also strong indirect signals from transport and forwarding system providers embedding AI in ETA forecasts, deviation management, and document interpretation. At the same time, it is not as mature as, for example, customer service or software development: many companies still use traditional systems, and adoption appears to be uneven between larger freight forwarders/3PL providers and smaller firms. Overall, the observable signals point to significant but not fully mainstream adoption, placing the profession at around 6/10.

description: Samordnar och bevakar order och leveranser genom kontakter med leverantörer, kunder, interna avdelningar, transportföretag, speditörer m.fl.
descriptionEnglish: Coordinates and monitors orders and deliveries through contacts with suppliers, customers, internal departments, transport companies, freight forwarders, etc.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for order coordinators in the coming year. This implies that individuals with the necessary education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but depending on the focus within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In the two-year outlook, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for order coordinators will be small to medium. Opportunities in the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 16300 yrkesverksamma ordersamordnare i Sverige, varav cirka 47 procent är kvinnor och 53 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Order coordinators primarily perform screen- and information-based work: monitoring orders and deliveries, communicating with suppliers, customers, internal departments, transport firms and freight forwarders, and resolving scheduling/logistics issues. A large share of the core task structure involves text, data, status tracking, routine coordination and exception handling, which AI systems can substantially assist with (e.g., inbox triage, ETA prediction, order status updates, documentation, and workflow automation). The role still requires human judgment, relationship handling and negotiation across organizations, so it is not near-maximal exposure, but it is clearly a majority digital/cognitive occupation with some interpersonal components.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Order coordinators work with order, delivery, and supplier communication, which involves a type of coordinating and text/case-based tasks where generative AI tools and automation support are already in practice. There are clear current signals in 2025–early 2026 for AI support in customer service, email responses, case prioritization, order status tracking, transport/freight communication, and simpler forecasting/deviation management through ERP/CRM integrations. At the same time, this is not a profession where one sees the same broad, standardized, and workflow-integrated AI usage as in, for example, development or law, and much of the work is still conducted in systems and routines where automation rather than full AI predominates. Overall, this indicates early but genuine adoption: some employers are experimenting with or using AI support, but it is not yet mainstream across the entire profession.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession primarily includes prominent and physical/entertainment-based tasks such as circus arts, juggling, imitation, dangerous stunt sequences, and program hosting in radio/TV. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3439 (other cultural and entertainment occupations), there are clear AI tools in use today, but adoption appears uneven and mostly experimental rather than standardized across employers. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is largely coordinating and knowledge-/documentation-based: reading scripts, planning practical details, noting lighting/effects/music, managing licensing and royalty issues, and ensuring that the right people and objects are in the right place at the right time. These are tasks where AI can provide strong support through scheduling, summarization, information retrieval, planning suggestions, and administration. At the same time, the role requires a significant amount of situationally adapted coordination in real-time with people, the dynamics of production, and physical presence in rehearsals/recordings/sets, which means that not everything can be automated. Overall: clearly above medium exposure to AI, but not nearly entirely digital.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For inspicienter och scriptor m.fl. there are clear AI-adjacent use cases, but current observable adoption in Sweden appears early rather than mainstream. In practice, these roles are heavily operational, context-specific, and dependent on live coordination, human judgment, and production workflows, which limits automation. Current AI signals are mostly from generic productivity tools: transcription/summarization for rehearsals and meetings, draft planning documents, translation/subtitling support, script annotation/search, and scheduling/communication assistance. Some film/TV and larger theatre/production organizations may be experimenting with these tools, but there is little evidence of widespread, occupation-specific deployment or hiring patterns comparable to higher-adoption office roles. Net: some active experimentation and tool use, but not yet broad, standardized adoption.

descriptionEnglish: Takes still and moving images for commercial, artistic, industrial, and scientific purposes. Works on image composition. Edits and assembles recorded material into finished programmes or films.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for photographers in the coming year. This means that individuals with the necessary education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but depending on the focus within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In the two-year outlook, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for photographers will be small to medium. Opportunities in the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 3700 yrkesverksamma fotografer i Sverige, varav cirka 36 procent är kvinnor och 64 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Photographers have a clear mix of creative, digital, and physical work tasks. AI can already assist significantly with image selection, editing, retouching, color correction, composition, metadata, and basic video editing, which enhances exposure. At the same time, the profession often requires physical presence, equipment handling, lighting, interaction with clients/customers, and adaptation to real environments, which AI cannot fully replace. Overall, it is a moderately exposed profession, but not nearly entirely digital.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
Current AI adoption signals for photographers are clearly present and practical, but not uniformly embedded across the occupation. By early 2026, many photographers and photo/video editors are using generative AI and AI-assisted tools for tasks like image enhancement, background removal, retouching, captioning, culling, transcription, and rough video editing. Major creative software ecosystems commonly used in Sweden—such as Adobe and similar suites—have integrated AI features that are actively used in day-to-day workflows. Swedish employers in media, marketing, e-commerce, and content production increasingly mention AI-assisted image/video production, which is a meaningful real-world signal.

That said, the occupation is still fundamentally driven by human capture, creative direction, client interaction, and visual judgment. AI is augmenting workflow more than replacing core work, and adoption is uneven across freelancers, small studios, agencies, and industrial/scientific photography. So this is above early experimentation and below full mainstream dependence, fitting a solid 6.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession combines significant cognitive tasks with strong human and situation-specific work. Treatment assistants and social pedagogues make assessments, document, plan interventions, follow up with clients, and work with collaboration between authorities and care. These aspects are partially exposed to AI through support for record-keeping, risk assessment, planning, and information retrieval. At the same time, core tasks require direct contact, trust-building relationships, conflict management, motivational interviewing, and adaptation to complex social and psychiatric situations, which AI cannot replace. In the Swedish welfare and healthcare context, the delegation of many decisions is also limited by regulations and professional responsibility. In summary: clear but medium AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For behandlingsassistenter och socialpedagoger finns some current AI signals, but they are still mostly limited and non-standardized. Observable adoption is more likely in adjacent administrative and documentation tasks than in the core client-facing work: drafting notes, summarizing case material, generating meeting documentation, translation/support for refugee-related work, and internal knowledge search. In Swedish public-sector and care settings, deployment is constrained by privacy, procurement, and safeguarding requirements, so widespread frontline AI use is not yet evident. As of early 2026, the occupation shows early experimentation and selective use rather than broad operational adoption, placing it around 3–4.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession consists largely of technical, digital, and knowledge-intensive work: measurement, design, calculations, drawings, control, and planning of operation/maintenance. These are tasks where AI can provide clear support through CAD/BIM analysis, sizing, review of documents, generation of drawing resources, and project support. At the same time, site-specific inspections, coordination with contractors, and control/final reviews in real construction and infrastructure projects require physical presence and contextual understanding. Overall, the exposure is high but not close to maximum since a significant part of the work is still practical and location-bound.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3112, there are clear but still limited contemporary AI signals. In Sweden, AI tools in construction/real estate are mainly used for drawing review, quantity take-offs/calculations, project planning, documentation, BIM support, error/deviation analysis, and basic text work related to work preparations and reports. There are also commercial solutions for generative design, image/document interpretation, and digital twins, but these are not yet widely standardized in the daily operations of construction and civil engineering professionals. The industry is relatively conservative, fragmented between small and large players, and often has lower actual AI penetration compared to, for example, IT, customer service, or marketing. At the same time, there are more advertisements and supplier initiatives, particularly among larger contractors, property companies, and technical consultants. Overall, this points to early adoption: some employers are experimenting, and certain tools are used in production, but it is not yet mainstream for the profession.

description: Övervakar person- och godstrafik vid rikets gränser och kontrollerar att tullbestämmelser efterlevs. Bevakar att tull, mervärdesskatt och andra avgifter betalas vid import. Övervakar sjötrafik samt säkerheten och miljöskyddet till sjöss. Kontrollerar att fiskeregler följs.
descriptionEnglish: Monitors personal and freight traffic at the country's borders and ensures compliance with customs regulations. Oversees the payment of customs, VAT, and other fees upon import. Monitors shipping traffic and safety as well as environmental protection at sea. Ensures adherence to fishing regulations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Customs and Coast Guard officials have a distinctly mixed job profile. A large part of the work is knowledge- and regulation-driven: document control, compliance with customs regulations, assessment of duties, risk analysis, and monitoring of traffic flows can be partially supported by AI, decision support, and automated review. At the same time, there is significant physical presence at borders, in ports, and at sea, involving direct control of people and goods, as well as exercising authority in situations where human judgment, authority, and responsibility are central. Therefore, the exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals for Swedish customs/coast guard officers appear to be in the early-adoption range rather than mainstream. There are observable uses of AI/analytics in border-security-adjacent work — for example risk scoring, anomaly detection in cargo/passenger flows, image analysis from scanners, surveillance support, and document triage — but these are typically embedded in larger inspection, intelligence, or enforcement systems rather than being a standard AI tool used directly by all officers. In Sweden, public-sector and enforcement adoption is slower than in private-sector knowledge work, and there is little evidence of widespread day-to-day generative AI use for core duties like inspections, reporting, or case handling across the occupation. Overall this points to some employers/units experimenting and selective deployment, but not yet broad, routine AI use.

description: Köper in varor och tjänster. Planerar och utreder inköpsbehov och tar in anbud och offerter. Upprätthåller och söker nya kontakter med leverantör. Gör sammanställningar och kalkyler, förhandlar om pris och leveransvillkor samt fattar beslut och skriver kontrakt och sluter avtal.
descriptionEnglish: Procures goods and services. Plans and evaluates purchasing needs and solicits bids and quotations. Maintains and seeks new contacts with suppliers. Compiles summaries and calculations, negotiates prices and delivery terms, and makes decisions and drafts contracts.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for purchasers and procurement officers in the coming year. This means that individuals with the necessary education or experience will have small to medium opportunities for work, but depending on the focus within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In the two-year outlook, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for purchasers and procurement officers will be significant. Opportunities in the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 19100 yrkesverksamma inköpare och upphandlare i Sverige, varav cirka 44 procent är kvinnor och 56 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is primarily cognitive and digital: needs analysis, tender procurement, comparisons, calculations, negotiation, contract drafting, and decision-making mostly occur through text, data, and systems. AI can significantly support searches, compilations, price analysis, draft contracts, and supplier assessments. At the same time, there are important human elements such as negotiations, relationship-building with suppliers, and accountable decision-making, which means it is not entirely automatable. In the Swedish context, procurement can also be rule-bound and organizationally sluggish, but the exposure to AI is nonetheless high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Buyers and procurement professionals show clear current AI adoption signals, but it is not yet near-universal. Observable use cases include AI-assisted tender/RFP drafting, contract clause review, supplier research, spend analysis, and summarizing procurement documents—especially in larger private organizations and some public-sector support functions. Procurement software ecosystems increasingly embed AI features, and job advertisements increasingly ask for digital/analytics skills rather than explicit AI, suggesting practical uptake. However, the work still relies heavily on negotiation, accountability, legal/compliance judgment, and stakeholder management, which limits full automation and keeps adoption uneven. In Sweden, adoption is likely strongest in larger firms and centralized procurement teams, while public-sector procurement remains more cautious. Overall, this reflects significant but not mainstream adoption.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Sales representatives primarily work with information gathering, customer contact, needs analysis, tailored offers, order management, and sales statistics. A significant portion of the work is digital and knowledge-based, making it relatively well-suited for AI support in areas such as lead generation, customer segmentation, proposal preparation, CRM updates, and forecasts. At the same time, the role requires relationship building, negotiation, proactive sales efforts, and customer-specific assessments in real business contexts, which limits full automation. Overall, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Company sales representatives show clear current AI adoption signals, but are not yet at full transformation. As of early 2026, many sales teams use AI for drafting outreach emails, summarizing customer meetings, generating call notes, updating CRM, prioritizing leads, and wording proposals/offers. Swedish employers increasingly mention AI/CRM automation in sales-related job ads, and mainstream tools are embedded in platforms like Microsoft, Salesforce, HubSpot, and other sales stacks used by Swedish firms. However, for this occupation, AI is still mostly assistive rather than core to the task, and adoption varies by company size and sales maturity. Most work still depends on relationship-building, negotiation, account knowledge, and district/product responsibility, which limits end-to-end automation. Therefore, the current signal indicates solid moderate adoption: widespread experimentation and meaningful use in many firms, but not universal or deeply autonomous.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for insurance sellers and insurance advisors in the coming year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Insurance sellers and insurance advisors primarily work with information gathering, needs analysis, risk assessment, premium calculation, terms review, and sales/advisory through digital systems. Much of the core work is document-, regulation-, and knowledge-intensive, making it well-suited for AI support that can compile materials, suggest products, assess risk patterns, and formulate customer-specific advice. At the same time, there are important elements of human selling, trust-building, as well as negotiations and compliance, which suggest that AI will likely complement rather than completely replace the role. Overall, there is a high theoretical exposure to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Insurance sellers and insurance advisors are showing clear but not yet fully established AI signals in Sweden by early 2026. There is widespread use of AI in related tasks such as customer communication, lead scoring, meeting notes, quote/policy drafting, chatbots, and simpler advisory support in insurance companies and brokerage environments. At the same time, the core of the role remains strongly human: needs analysis, advisory services, trust-building sales, risk assessment, and condition evaluation, which means that AI is more often used as support rather than as a replacement. The industry also exhibits some regulatory and quality-related caution, which slows down full-scale automation. Overall, the observable signals indicate early but genuine adoption rather than broad, standardized use.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for claims adjusters and appraisers in the coming year. scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is primarily knowledge- and office-based: handling insurance claims, investigating damages, inspecting and appraising, estimating compensation, and documenting decisions. Much of the core work is based on information gathering, regulations, assessments, and written documentation that AI can support well, particularly in analysis, comparison against insurance terms, and automation of routine decisions. At the same time, there is a certain physical and interpersonal aspect in damage assessment, customer contact, and situational evaluation on-site, which means that exposure is not maximized. Overall, there is a high but not total exposure to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For claims adjusters and appraisers, there are clear but still primarily early/adopter AI signals, with not fully realized impact. In practice, AI is used in certain insurance companies for document reading, case classification, damage assessment, fraud detection, image analysis (e.g., vehicle damage), and support for responses/drafts in customer and case management. At the same time, the role is heavily regulated, assessments require human accountability, and much of the work consists of investigation, negotiation, and valuation where AI is more often a decision support tool than an autonomous solution. In Sweden, adoption is mainly seen in larger companies and in pilot/scaled subprocesses, while broad standardization is still lacking. Overall: clear current adoption signals, but still at an early to moderate level.

descriptionEnglish: Leads and allocates work on site. Ensures that machinery and materials are on site and coordinates work with subcontractors. forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Foremen in construction, civil engineering, and mining have a mix of administrative/coordinating and practical tasks. The role involves planning, following up on drawings and specifications, coordinating with subcontractors, monitoring materials and machinery, as well as certain personnel responsibilities. These aspects present moderate AI exposure since AI can assist in planning, scheduling, documentation, quality, and safety checks. At the same time, the work is heavily site-specific, requires presence on the job site, ongoing real-time decision-making, safety responsibilities, and human management of team collaboration, which limits automation. Overall: clearly defined but not highly exposed, therefore mid-level.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For construction, civil engineering, and mining supervisors, there are visible current AI signals, but they are still mostly early and assistive rather than core to the role. In practice, employers increasingly use AI-enabled tools for scheduling, project coordination, document drafting, safety/compliance checklists, BIM/engineering support, image-based progress reporting, and sometimes drone/computer-vision inspection or predictive maintenance around machinery and materials. These tools are observable in construction technology and Swedish enterprise software, and job ads across adjacent roles increasingly mention digital/AI literacy. However, the occupation itself is not yet broadly transformed by AI: day-to-day work remains highly site-based, dependent on supervision, coordination, safety enforcement, and interaction with crews and subcontractors. Adoption is therefore more than marginal but not yet mainstream, fitting early adoption rather than strong widespread deployment.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is clearly mixed: a large part of the work consists of technical design, drawing and calculation work, planning, documentation, testing, and coordination, which is relatively exposed to AI. At the same time, practical work involving assembly, maintenance, repair, testing, and inspection of machines and mechanical equipment is included, which requires physical presence and situational assessment on-site. Overall, the exposure lies in the middle of the scale: AI can assist significantly in analysis, design, troubleshooting, and documentation, but cannot replace the practical and workshop-related aspects.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3114, there are clear but still limited current AI signals: CAD/CAE tools, generative AI for drawing foundations, documentation, troubleshooting, and maintenance planning are present among some employers, and in larger industrial companies, AI support is used for simulation, quality control, and production optimization. At the same time, the core work of the profession is strongly connected to physical equipment, site-bound processes, accountability, and domain-specific engineering assessments, which means AI functions more as a support rather than a broadly integrated workspace. Therefore, there are more than marginal pilot cases, but adoption is not yet mainstream across the entire profession. Thus, the assessment lands on early but clear adoption rather than significant widespread implementation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Brokers in finance primarily engage in digital, knowledge-intensive trading and advising concerning securities, currencies, and derivatives. The core tasks consist of analyzing market data, making buy/sell decisions, executing orders, and communicating recommendations—all of which AI models are well-suited to support or partially automate. While there are certain human elements such as client relations, accountability, and regulatory frameworks, the actual work content is almost entirely screen- and information-based, providing a very high theoretical exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For financial brokers, there are clear, observable AI signals today: many trading, banking, and brokerage organizations are already using AI/ML for market surveillance, signal generation, risk and portfolio analysis, customer segmentation, document analysis, and internal research. In Sweden, the adoption is not always as visible as in, for example, IT, but within the regulated financial sector, it is nonetheless quite established among larger players. At the same time, the core task of executing clients' securities transactions is still strongly human-driven and tightly regulated, which means that AI primarily supports rather than replaces the role. Overall, this corresponds to a high but not maximal level of current adoption.

nameEnglish: Technicians in Diagnostic Imaging and Medical Equipment
description: Installerar, kontrollerar, övervakar och utför service på medicinteknisk utrustning som används vid diagnosticering eller behandling av olika sjukdomar. Sköter och övervakar t.ex. hjärtlungmaskiner.
descriptionEnglish: Installs, checks, monitors, and performs service on medical equipment used for diagnosing or treating various diseases. Maintains and monitors, for example, heart-lung machines.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The work is mixed. Technicians in medical imaging and medical device technology perform both practical, site-based tasks such as installation, service, inspection, and monitoring of medical equipment, as well as more cognitive tasks like troubleshooting, documentation, and system oversight. AI can likely support fault diagnosis, service planning, and monitoring through data analysis, but it cannot replace the physical work, safety responsibilities, and clinical context on site. Therefore, the theoretical exposure is moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3211 (tekniker inom bilddiagnostik och medicinteknisk utrustning), there are some observable AI-related signals in early 2026, but they are still limited and not yet mainstream. In practice, AI in this occupation is mostly appearing indirectly through AI-enabled medical devices, predictive maintenance, decision-support features in imaging systems, and vendor-specific monitoring/quality tools rather than as day-to-day standalone AI workflows for technicians. Swedish hospitals and public healthcare organizations are adopting AI more slowly than private sectors, and this role is tightly regulated, safety-critical, and equipment-dependent, which slows broad deployment. There are pilots and some operational use of AI in imaging departments, but the technician role itself is not showing strong, widespread direct AI adoption yet. So this is best rated as marginal-to-early adoption, around 2.5/10.

description: Utför kliniska analyser vid sjukhus, läkemedelsindustrin eller laboratorium. Gör kemiska och biokemiska analyser samt tar prover. Hanterar teknisk apparatur och annan utrustning för provtagning och analys. Tar fram underlag som läkare behöver för att fastställa rätt diagnos. Deltar i forskning och utvecklingsarbete. Genomför analyser och dokumenterar bevis vid brottsutredningar.
descriptionEnglish: Performs clinical analyses at hospitals, in the pharmaceutical industry, or laboratories. Conducts chemical and biochemical analyses and takes samples. Operates technical apparatus and other equipment for sampling and analysis. Provides materials needed by doctors to establish correct diagnoses. Participates in research and development. Conducts analyses and documents evidence in criminal investigations.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for biomedical analysts in the coming year. This means that individuals who have the required education or experience will have very large job opportunities, but depending on the specialization within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for biomedical analysts will be very significant. Opportunities within the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 9500 yrkesverksamma biomedicinska analytiker i Sverige, varav cirka 85 procent är kvinnor och 15 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Biomedical analysts perform a mix of laboratory work and knowledge-intensive analyses. Part of the work is clearly digital and cognitive: interpreting analysis results, documenting, compiling materials for diagnosis, supporting research, and managing data. Here, AI can likely provide significant support in pattern recognition, quality control, report generation, and decision support. At the same time, the profession is also strongly tied to physical sample handling, instrument work, sampling, quality assurance in a laboratory environment, and sometimes forensic elements, which require human presence and responsibility. Overall, the exposure level is medium to quite high, but not as high as for entirely digital knowledge jobs.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For biomedicinska analytiker m.fl. there are clear but still limited current AI adoption signals. In laboratory and clinical pathology settings, AI is increasingly used for image-based pre-screening, quality control, workflow triage, and pattern detection in areas like microscopy, hematology, and some molecular diagnostics. There are also emerging AI tools in research and industrial lab environments for data analysis and automation support. However, as of early 2026 this is not yet mainstream across the occupation in Sweden: much of the work remains tightly regulated, instrument-driven, and dependent on validated methods, human sign-off, and chain-of-custody requirements in forensic contexts. Public healthcare adoption is slower, and many Swedish employers are still in pilot or selective-use stages rather than broad deployment. Overall this indicates early adoption with some operational use, but not yet widespread enough for a higher score.

description: Bereder, expedierar, marknadsför och kvalitetskontrollerar läkemedel inom exempelvis apotek, läkemedelsindustri och sjukhus. Ger råd om läkemedel och hälsa.
descriptionEnglish: Compounds, dispenses, markets, and quality-controls medications within pharmacies, the pharmaceutical industry, and hospitals. Provides advice on medications and health.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for pharmacists in the coming year. This means that individuals who have the required education or experience will have significant job opportunities, but depending on the specialization within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for pharmacists will be significant. Opportunities within the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Bedömningen för farmaceuter är en samlad bedömning för receptarier, apotekare. Det finns cirka 6600 yrkesverksamma farmaceuter i Sverige, varav cirka 87 procent är kvinnor och 13 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Pharmacists primarily work with knowledge- and information-intensive tasks: preparation, dispensing, quality control, pharmaceutical information, counseling, and some marketing. Much of the work is conducted digitally or relies on rule-based decisions where AI can assist with monitoring, documentation, inventory/forecasting, interaction checks, and customer counseling support. At the same time, there are important elements of physical handling of medications, regulatory responsibilities, and personal interaction and professional judgment that cannot be fully automated, especially in pharmacies and care-related environments. Overall, the exposure is high but not nearly total.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For receptarier (pharmacists/pharmacy technicians in Swedish practice), there are clear current AI-adoption signals but they are still mostly early-stage rather than mainstream. Observable use exists in adjacent workflows: pharmacy customer service chatbots, AI-supported knowledge search for drug information, automated prescription handling/triage, and AI-driven quality/safety monitoring in parts of the pharmaceutical industry and hospital pharmacy. Swedish employers have also been increasing mentions of AI tools in job ads generally, and pharmacy-related organizations are beginning to experiment with decision support and documentation assistance.

However, as of early 2026 there is not strong evidence that AI is broadly embedded in day-to-day receptarie work across Swedish pharmacies or public hospitals. The core tasks—dispensing, checking prescriptions, counseling patients, and ensuring legal/medication safety—remain heavily regulated and require human accountability, which slows deployment. In public-sector and healthcare pharmacy settings especially, adoption appears cautious and limited to pilots or supportive tools rather than widespread production use.

Overall this fits early adoption: some employers are experimenting and a few tools are in use, but AI is not yet mainstream for the occupation.

descriptionEnglish: Responsible for the operation and maintenance of mechanical, electrical, and electronic equipment onboard ships.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Marine officers work with the operation, monitoring, troubleshooting, and maintenance of mechanical, electrical, and electronic equipment onboard ships. The role is clearly technical and includes planning, documentation, diagnostics, and decision-making that AI can support, particularly through monitoring systems, predictive maintenance, and fault analysis. At the same time, the work takes place in a physical, safety-critical, and strongly context-dependent environment at sea, where practical interventions, on-site responsibility, and collaboration with the crew cannot be fully automated. Overall, the exposure is moderate to medium-high, but not predominantly digital.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For machine officers, there are certain clear signals of digitalization and automation onboard, but much weaker and more indirect AI signals compared to office professions. In practice, today's tools primarily focus on condition monitoring, predictive maintenance, alarm systems, documentation, energy optimization, and remote support from shipping companies/suppliers — often involving rule-based analysis and traditional automation rather than broadly implemented generative AI in daily work. There are pilot projects and vendor solutions within shipping for AI-based maintenance forecasting and operational optimization, but in Swedish shipping companies, this is still not a clearly mainstream tool for machine officers. Therefore, the current adoption is assessed as marginal to early pilot level, rather than established use.

description: Samordnar, leder och följer upp arbete vid tillverkningsprocesser.
descriptionEnglish: Coordinates, leads, and follows up on work during manufacturing processes.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Production supervisors have a clear blend of digital and organizational tasks: planning production, monitoring flows, reporting, staffing, and communicating with operators and other functions. Much of this is information- and decision-intensive and can be supported by AI through scheduling, deviation analysis, production optimization, and report generation. At the same time, the role requires a presence in production, quick situational assessment, on-site leadership, and coordination with people and machines in a physical environment, which limits full automation. Therefore, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For manufacturing team leaders, there are clear but still limited current AI signals. In Sweden, AI is primarily used in related functions such as planning, quality control, predictive maintenance, scheduling, deviation management, and report writing, often through MES/ERP, BI, and production systems with built-in AI features rather than standalone AI tools. There is also some usage of generative AI tools for meeting notes, instructions, and communication. However, it is not a profession where AI is already standard in the daily workflow of the majority of employers; adoption still seems to be early and uneven between larger industrial companies and smaller factories. Therefore, it is categorized as early adoption rather than mainstream.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession primarily consists of technical and cognitive work: construction, drawing, and calculation tasks, coordination of manufacturing, as well as quality and product controls. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 3119, there are clear but still not fully widespread adoption signals in Sweden as of early 2026. At the same time, this is not a profession with as mature and standardized AI flows as, for instance, software development or customer service. description: Utför karttekniskt arbete. Utför utredningar och beräkningar samt sammanställer och analyserar mätdata. Framställer kartor av olika slag.
descriptionEnglish: Performs cartographic work. Conducts surveys and calculations, compiles, and analyzes measurement data. Produces various types of maps.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: GIS and cartographic engineers primarily work with digital and knowledge-intensive processing of measurement data, investigations, calculations, and map production. A significant portion of the work consists of analysis, compilation, and production of maps and geographic information, which AI models can effectively support through data processing, pattern recognition, automated map production, and decision support. At the same time, there are elements of quality control, method selection, client/business adaptation, and sometimes collaboration with other stakeholders, which means that certain aspects require human judgment. Overall, the profession is clearly digital and cognitive, but not entirely automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: GIS and cartographic engineers have distinct AI-relevant tasks (geodata analysis, classification, cartography, image interpretation, data quality), and there are currently commercial tools with AI support within GIS/CAD/remote sensing that are used in certain organizations. However, observable current adoption signals in Sweden are still largely early-stage: many employers are experimenting with AI for the automation of geoprocesses, object recognition in satellite/drone images, and simpler generative support tools, but it does not yet appear to be a widespread standard in the profession. Additionally, the public sector, where many GIS services exist, tends to implement AI more slowly. Overall: clear but not mainstream signals that are best suited to the early adoption interval.

Civilingenjörsyrken inom gruvteknik och metallurgi (2146)
descriptionEnglish: Performs technical work related to the extraction of metals, oil, and gas or in the production of metals, alloys, and metallurgical products, as well as in the maintenance and repair of mining installations. Conducts quality and product controls related to steel and metal production.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is diverse but has a clear core of technical and analytical work that AI can support: process monitoring, quality control, troubleshooting, reporting, planning, and optimization within mining and metallurgy production. At the same time, much of the work is site-specific, including inspections, maintenance and repair work at facilities, as well as collaboration with operational staff, which means that AI cannot replace the entire task. Overall, this results in a medium theoretical exposure rather than high, as a large part of the work is digital/cognitive but involves significant physical and context-dependent elements.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: There are certain observable AI signals for this profession, but they appear to be limited and not widely established in daily work. In mining engineering and metallurgy, AI/ML is primarily used in adjacent areas such as process optimization, predictive maintenance, quality control with image analysis, production planning, and monitoring of machinery/sensors. Such tools are likely found at larger industrial companies and suppliers, but the professional role is still strongly dominated by traditional process, operations, and quality management rather than generative AI in the workflow. Therefore, I see this as early adoption: some companies are experimenting or using targeted interventions, but there are no strong signs that AI is a standard tool for the majority of engineers and technicians in mining engineering and metallurgy in Sweden by the start of 2026.

nameSwedish: Ingenjörer och tekniker inom kemi och kemiteknik
nameEnglish: Engineers and Technicians in Chemistry and Chemical Engineering
description:
Utför tekniskt arbete i samband med kemiteknisk produktion. Organiserar, leder och samordnar det praktiska arbetet vid tillverkning av petroleumprodukter, kemikalier, kemiska och kemitekniska produkter, läkemedel, syntetiska material eller livsmedel. Utför kvalitets- och produktionskontroller av kemiska och kemitekniska produkter under produktionsprocessen.
 
Hit hör inte
Civilingenjörsyrken inom kemi och kemiteknik (2145)
descriptionEnglish: Performs technical work related to chemical engineering production. Organizes, leads, and coordinates practical work in the manufacture of petroleum products, chemicals, chemical and chemical engineering products, pharmaceuticals, synthetic materials, or foodstuffs. Performs quality and production controls of chemical and chemical engineering products during the production process.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for engineers and technicians in chemistry and chemical engineering in the coming year. This means that individuals who have the required education or experience will have small to medium job opportunities, but depending on the specialization within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for engineers and technicians in chemistry and chemical engineering will be small to medium. Opportunities within the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.
forecast.ingress: Det finns cirka 3200 yrkesverksamma ingenjörer och tekniker inom kemi och kemiteknik i Sverige, varav cirka 36 procent är kvinnor och 64 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession is a blend of technical-cognitive and practical elements. A large part of the work involves monitoring processes, quality controls, production planning, documentation, and coordination, which are relatively exposed to AI through analysis, decision support, and automation. At the same time, practical work in a production environment is included, close to process equipment and often in collaboration with operators, which means that AI cannot fully replace the core functions. Overall: clear but not high exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale:
For SSYK 3115 (engineers and technicians in chemistry and chemical engineering), there are clear but still primarily early adoption signals. In practice, AI/ML is already being used in certain chemical processes and quality controls, for example, for process monitoring, anomaly detection, predictive maintenance, recipe/batch optimization, and vision-based inspection in production. There is also an increasing prevalence of AI in laboratory and production environments through supplier systems, MES/SCADA integration, and industrial analytics platforms.

At the same time, this is not a profession where general AI assistants are as established as in software development or customer service. The adoption is uneven across larger process industries, pharmaceuticals, food production, and smaller manufacturers, with much taking place as pilot projects or in limited parts of operations rather than as widely standardized workflows. Regulatory requirements, safety, validation, and accountability in production also hinder rapid rollout.

Overall, observable signals indicate early adoption with some actual usage in larger and more digitally mature companies, but it is not yet mainstream across the profession as a whole.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The profession consists of a mixture of administrative/investigative social work and direct human contact. Examples such as receiving and introducing newcomers, personal investigations, and case transition require documentation, assessments, planning, and informational work where AI can provide clear support. At the same time, the work is highly dependent on relationships, trust, authority exercise, individual assessments, and collaboration in real situations, which limits automation. In the Swedish public/municipal context and with state-regulated tasks, practical AI exposure is therefore moderate rather than high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 2669 (other social work roles), there are observable AI signals in Sweden, but they are still mostly early and uneven. The occupation includes municipal intake/introductory work for newcomers and case assessment/reintegration work, where generative AI can assist with drafting, summarizing, translation, and administrative documentation. However, in the Swedish public sector these roles are constrained by confidentiality, legal accountability, and limited integration into core case-management systems. As of early 2026, there are some employer pilots and individual use of general-purpose AI tools, but no strong evidence of widespread, routine AI-assisted workflows across this occupation. So adoption is real but not mainstream.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Clergy have a significant share of knowledge and text-based tasks that are well-suited for AI support: preparation of sermons, text interpretation, teaching on matters of faith, planning congregational work, as well as administration and communication. In the Swedish context, ecclesiastical activities are also often cautious with digital support systems, and there are no strong indications that AI has been established as a standard tool for priests. Overall, this points to marginal adoption: isolated attempts or individual usage, but little to suggest widespread implementation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Directors and producers of film, theater, etc., primarily engage in creative, planning, and decision-making tasks that AI can significantly influence: script and concept development, idea generation, scheduling, budget support, analysis of material, and preliminary production proposals. In Swedish theater and public-service-adjacent production, adoption is slower and more cautious due to budget, rights, union, and artistic concerns. Overall this looks like early adoption with some practical deployment, not yet broad routine use across the occupation.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Musicians, singers, and composers have a distinctly mixed work profile. The creative aspects of the job, particularly composition, arranging, practice planning, notation, and simpler production, are relatively exposed to AI, as models can generate music ideas, accompaniments, lyrics, and sheet music. At the same time, the essence of many assignments lies in live performances, interpretation, interaction with other musicians, conducting, and artistic expression in front of an audience, which is difficult to automate. In Sweden, the practical compensation from AI is also limited by strong collective agreements and the fact that many assignments are performance- and relationship-based. Overall, this results in a medium level of exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For musicians, singers, and composers in Sweden, there are clear AI tools in use today for ideation, demo generation, arrangement support, voice cleanup, mastering, transcription, and marketing content, but these are still mostly auxiliary rather than core workflow tools. Observable adoption signals are strongest among independent creators, studios, media producers, and some orchestral/choir administration use cases, while live performance itself remains largely human-led. Job ads and public employer demand for explicit AI skills in this occupation are limited compared with knowledge-work occupations, and there is little evidence of broad, standardized deployment across Swedish music employers. Overall this looks like early adoption with some experimentation, not mainstream transformation.

scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For translators, interpreters and linguists, there are strong observable AI adoption signals by early 2026, but not full mainstream replacement-level deployment. Machine translation, LLM-based drafting, terminology extraction, speech-to-text, subtitle/localization support, and post-editing workflows are now widely available and actively used in many translation agencies, localization vendors, media firms, and parts of the public sector. Swedish employers increasingly mention AI-assisted translation, terminology work, and language review in job ads and workflows, indicating real operational use rather than only experimentation. However, adoption is still uneven: high-stakes legal, medical, public-sector, and sworn interpreting contexts often retain heavy human oversight, and many linguist/language-research tasks are not yet broadly automated. Overall this fits significant adoption with many employers using tools, but not near-universal or fully mature deployment.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The journalism profession is highly cognitive and digital: collecting, compiling, writing, editing, fact-checking, and monitoring news, as well as producing comments and analyses. Much of the work involves text, information, research, and publishing, which is well-suited for AI support in ideation, transcription, summarization, translation, monitoring, and draft production. At the same time, the role requires human judgment, source criticism, ethical considerations, interviews, and editorial responsibility, which means that AI usually assists rather than replaces the entire profession. The overall exposure is therefore assessed to be high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current AI adoption for journalists in Sweden appears to be in the significant-but-not-universal range. Observable signals by early 2026 include newsroom use of generative AI for drafting, summarizing, headline suggestions, transcription, translation, and research support, with some Swedish media organizations openly discussing internal AI guidelines and experimentation. However, the occupation still relies heavily on human sourcing, editorial judgment, verification, and accountability, and adoption is uneven across employers and especially slower in public-service and smaller newsrooms. This supports a score in the 6–7 band rather than a fully mainstream 8+ level.

nameSwedish: Arkeologer och specialister inom humaniora m.m.
description: Studerar människors kultur och livsbetingelser med hjälp av fynd och föremål från forntida boplatser, gravar m.m. Leder och organiserar arkeologiska utgrävningar. Studerar och tolkar urkunder, sociala samt rumsliga fenomen. Utvecklar teorier, modeller och metoder, bl.a. för att tolka och beskriva händelser, kulturella och ekonomiska förhållanden. Genomför experiment, analyserar och värderar data. Undersöker sociala strukturer och beteenden hos olika grupper i samhället, föreslår och värderar utfallet av socialpolitiska åtgärder och utvecklar teorier och modeller för politiska system och institutioner.
descriptionEnglish: Study human culture and living conditions using finds and objects from ancient settlements, graves, etc. Lead and organise archaeological excavations. Study and interpret primary sources, social, and spatial phenomena. Develop theories, models, and methods, among others to interpret and describe events, cultural and economic conditions. Conduct experiments, analyse, and evaluate data. Investigate social structures and behaviours in various community groups, propose and assess the results of social policy measures, and develop theories and models for political systems and institutions.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Archaeologists and specialists in the humanities primarily work with knowledge- and analysis-intensive tasks: interpreting finds, analyzing data, writing reports, developing theories and models, as well as evaluating cultural and social phenomena. Much of the core work is text-, information-, and decision-based, which AI is well-suited to support through searching, compiling, pattern recognition, preliminary interpretations, and report drafts. At the same time, there are certain physical and context-dependent elements, particularly during excavations, fieldwork, and assessments that require expert judgment on-site. Overall, the exposure is high since the majority of the work content is digital and cognitive, but not fully automatable.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish archaeologists and related humanities/social-science specialists, current AI adoption appears to be in early use rather than mainstream. Observable signals likely include occasional use of LLMs for literature review, drafting, translation, coding/statistical support, transcription, and some image/data analysis in research settings, but not widespread, occupation-specific AI tooling embedded in everyday workflows. In archaeology, specialized digital methods (GIS, photogrammetry, pattern recognition) exist, and some AI-assisted analysis may be used in projects, yet deployment is uneven and typically project-driven. In the humanities and social-science side, AI is increasingly available but still more often exploratory than fully integrated, especially in Sweden’s relatively cautious public/academic sector. Overall this fits a 4–5 range: real but still early adoption signals, not broad mainstream use.

descriptionEnglish: Plan and organise library activities. Assess, purchase, and classify books, journals, and other types of published material. Offer in-depth literature and information dissemination and guide library users in information retrieval. Search, process, and present information. Collect, investigate, assess, and order documents. Evaluate and determine which information should be preserved for the future.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Employment Agency assesses that there will be competition for jobs for librarians and archivists in the coming year. This means that individuals who have the required education or experience will have small to medium job opportunities, but depending on the specialization within the profession, the labor market may vary.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In a two-year perspective, the Employment Agency assesses that job opportunities for librarians and archivists will be significant. Opportunities within the profession increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional licenses.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Librarians and archivists primarily work with information retrieval, selection, classification, cataloging, compilation, and assessment of materials—mainly cognitive and digital tasks where AI is already strong. AI can streamline metadata work, searching, summarization, recommendations, indexing, and parts of archival appraisal. At the same time, there are important human elements such as user guidance, professional assessment of preservation value, local context, and quality control, but the core tasks are clearly knowledge- and text-intensive.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish librarians and archivists, current AI adoption signals look like early adoption rather than mainstream use. There are observable tools and pilots for tasks like metadata enrichment, OCR, search/discovery, automatic classification, transcription, and generative drafting of reference responses or user guidance, especially in larger libraries, university libraries, and some archive environments. However, as of early 2026 these uses are not yet broadly standardized across employers. Public-sector and municipal settings remain cautious because of privacy, recordkeeping, procurement, and archival integrity requirements, which slow deployment. Overall the occupation shows some real operational AI use, but the majority of roles are still more manual than AI-enabled, placing it around 4–5 rather than 6+.

At the same time, it is not common for AI to replace core tasks such as prosecution decisions, litigation, or evidence evaluation. Overall, there are observable but limited adoption signals today, which places the profession in early adoption rather than mainstream.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: SSYK 2519 covers highly knowledge-intensive IT specialist work such as planning IT strategies and conducting IT research. The core tasks are largely screen-based, analytical, and information-processing oriented, which are exactly the kinds of tasks AI can strongly assist with through drafting, analysis, coding support, system design, and research synthesis. While human judgment, organizational context, and accountability remain important, the occupation’s task structure is predominantly digital and cognitively intensive, so theoretical AI exposure is very high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 2519 (other IT specialists), there are clear, observable signs of adoption at the beginning of 2026. The role includes, among other things, IT strategy, technology development, and IT research, where AI tools are already widely used to support analysis, ideation, documentation, prototyping, code and solution design, as well as knowledge search. In the Swedish labor market, there is also an increasing mention of AI in job advertisements, and many employers expect practical use of generative AI tools or AI support in IT-related specialist roles. At the same time, adoption is not entirely uniform or standardized: usage varies between private and public employers, and for more strategic or research-related tasks, AI is often a support rather than a fully integrated core process. Overall, this indicates significant but still uneven adoption, suggesting a score around 6–7 rather than 8–10.

descriptionEnglish: Responsible for IT security in companies and organisations. Conduct risk and vulnerability assessments. Design security solutions and IT security systems based on the needs of the organisation.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: IT security specialists primarily engage in digital and knowledge-intensive tasks: risk and vulnerability analyses, design of security solutions, monitoring, incident management, and policy work. Most of the work involves text, data, and decision-making, where AI can support analysis, monitoring, reporting, and proposals for actions. However, the role also has important human elements such as dialogue requirements with the organization, prioritization, accountability, and adaptation to the organization's risk profile, which means it is not entirely automatable. Overall, the exposure is high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: IT security specialists are seeing strong current AI adoption signals, though not yet at the level of fully routine use across all employers. In early 2026, many security teams use AI-enabled SIEM/SOAR features, EDR/XDR platforms, threat-intelligence triage, phishing analysis, log summarization, alert clustering, and some LLM-based assistance for policy writing, incident documentation, and vulnerability prioritization. Swedish employers—especially larger private firms, consultancies, and managed security providers—are increasingly adopting these tools, and job ads commonly reference AI, automation, or security orchestration in practice rather than just as future interest. That said, usage is still uneven due to strict confidentiality, data residency, and trust requirements, and public-sector adoption is typically slower. So the role shows significant real-world adoption, but not universal mainstream dependence.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be little competition for jobs for system administrators in the coming year. This means that individuals with the required education or experience will have great opportunities for employment, but the job market may vary depending on the focus within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: Over a two-year period, the Swedish Public Employment Service estimates that job prospects for system administrators will be significant. Employment opportunities in this field increase if you have relevant certifications, qualifications, or professional proof.
