descriptionEnglish: Formulates and is responsible for goals and strategies in purchasing, logistics, and transport activities. Develops guidelines, monitors, and evaluates operations against established goals for purchasing, storage, transportation, and distribution. Participates in negotiations with suppliers, is responsible for inventory management, and manages the handling of freight. Responsible for budgeting and resource allocation within the operations. Holds personnel responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This occupation is largely managerial and knowledge-based: setting goals and strategies, developing policies, monitoring performance, budgeting, resource allocation, negotiations with suppliers, and personnel responsibility. These are tasks where AI can strongly assist with analysis, reporting, forecasting, scenario planning, document drafting, and decision support. The role still includes interpersonal negotiation, accountability, and operational oversight that require human judgment, so it is not near-fully automatable. Overall, the core task mix is predominantly digital/cognitive with some relational and real-world operational components, placing it in the 7–8 range.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For purchasing, logistics, and transport managers, there are clear but still uneven current adoption signals in Sweden. AI is already being used in practice for forecasting, route optimization, inventory planning, procurement analysis, anomaly detection, and document/contract support, particularly in larger private companies, transport operators, and retail. At the same time, there is not yet a wide, standardized level of daily AI usage across the entire profession, and much of the work still relies on negotiation, personnel management, operational responsibility, and business governance where AI primarily serves as decision support. In the Swedish context, we therefore see more than just pilot-level initiatives, but not a full-scale implementation among the majority of employers. Consequently, the profession is at a clear early-maturity level around 5.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: IT managers primarily work with digital and knowledge-intensive tasks: goal and strategy formulation, governance, monitoring, budgeting, resource allocation, IT security, and personnel management. Much of the work relies on analysis, documentation, decision support, and communication, making it relatively exposed to AI. At the same time, the role often requires organizational responsibility, negotiations, leadership, and adaptation to the specific context of the organization, which limits full automation. Overall, the exposure is high but not maximal.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish IT managers (IT-chefer), there are strong current adoption signals but not usually as direct end-user AI tooling as in developer or customer-service roles. By early 2026, many employers have visible use of AI in IT operations, service management, security monitoring, procurement support, reporting, policy drafting, and budget/strategy work. Common signals include use of Microsoft Copilot / M365 AI features, AI-enabled ITSM and endpoint/security platforms, AI-assisted analytics, and management-level drafting/summarization for plans, risk assessments, and documentation. Job ads and employer communications increasingly mention AI governance, AI strategy, and responsible use in IT leadership roles. However, adoption is uneven across sectors, and public-sector/municipal IT leadership remains slower and more cautious. Overall this is beyond early experimentation and into significant but still uneven practical adoption, so a 6 is appropriate.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The occupation is primarily managerial and administrative: setting goals and strategy, drafting guidelines, following up and evaluating activities, managing budgets, and allocating resources. These are largely screen- and knowledge-based tasks where AI can strongly assist with analysis, reporting, planning, forecasting, and document drafting. The role also includes personnel responsibility and likely coordination with people and stakeholders, which limits full automation, but the core work is still highly digital and cognitively oriented. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 1290 (other administrative and service managers), there are clear yet still limited current AI signals. In practice, AI is primarily used as support in administrative managerial tasks: meeting notes, drafts of reports/memos, analysis of budget and follow-up materials, internal communication, and simpler decision support in operational management. There is also some use of AI in HR and resource planning, case management, and service processes, particularly in larger private organizations and certain municipal/administrative-related operations. However, this is not a role where AI is deeply integrated into core work in the same way as in, for example, development or customer service. The adoption level in Sweden is therefore best described as early but real: several employers are experimenting with or using generative tools in parts of managerial work, but it is not yet mainstream across the entire professional group, particularly not in the public sector.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Sales and marketing managers primarily work with strategy, budgeting, pricing, campaigns, tracking, and resource allocation — predominantly cognitive and digital tasks where AI can support analysis, forecasting, reporting, and decision-making. scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Current adoption signals are significant for sales and marketing managers in Sweden, but not uniformly embedded across all employers. descriptionEnglish: Responsible for strategic HR issues such as workforce planning, recruitment, and personnel development. Responsible for salary policy and pay negotiations. Oversees workplace health and social issues as well as the interpretation of employment laws and agreements. Develops guidelines, monitors, and evaluates operations against established goals.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The role is strongly knowledge- and screen-based: personnel planning, recruitment, payroll policy, negotiations, labor law interpretation, guidelines, follow-up, and evaluation are all tasks where AI can support analysis, document preparation, policy drafts, compilations, and decision-making materials. At the same time, there are important human and organizational elements such as wage negotiations, union cooperation, leadership, and context-dependent assessments, which means that AI is likely to assist rather than replace the core role. In a Swedish context, collective agreements, labor law, and union processes may also hinder implementation, but the nature of the work is still clearly highly exposed to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish personal- och HR-chefer there are clear current adoption signals, but not yet universal mainstream transformation. By early 2026, many employers are using AI in parts of HR work: drafting job ads and policies, summarising candidate materials, supporting recruitment screening, handling employee Q&A via chatbots, analysing workforce data, and assisting with HR document creation and translation. These are observable tooling uses rather than just future potential. However, the role is also constrained by legal, labour-relations, and privacy requirements (GDPR, employment law, union negotiations), which limit full automation and slow adoption compared with some office functions. In Sweden, larger private employers and shared-service HR teams are more likely to have adopted such tools, while public-sector HR remains more cautious. Overall this fits significant but uneven adoption, so a 6 is appropriate.

description: Ansvarar för att upprätta budget samt för redovisning och bokslut. Kontrollerar att verksamhetens resurser används på ett effektivt sätt. Utformar riktlinjer, följer upp och utvärderar verksamheten mot uppsatta ekonomiska mål. Redovisar resultat och rapporterar till företagsledningen eller motsvarande. Har personalansvar.
descriptionEnglish: Responsible for budget preparation as well as accounting and financial statements. Ensures that the organization's resources are used effectively. Develops guidelines, monitors, and evaluates operations against established financial goals. Reports results to management or equivalent. Holds personnel responsibility.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Chief Financial Officers primarily work with budgeting, accounting, financial statements, monitoring, analysis, reporting, and guidelines — mainly involving digital and knowledge-intensive tasks where AI can already assist with analysis, draft reports, deviation identification, and forecasts. However, the role also includes personnel responsibility, leadership, prioritization, and accountability in an organizational context that requires human judgment and grounding. Overall, the exposure is high, but not entirely maximal since decisions, responsibilities, and internal governance cannot be fully automated.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: Economy and finance executives in Sweden are showing clear but not yet fully comprehensive signals of AI adoption. There is already broad use of AI-supported tools in finance and reporting tasks: automated variance analysis, forecasting, budget preparation, summarization of management reports, consolidated reporting, and simpler text production in governance documents. Many larger companies and consultant-driven finance functions are using or testing built-in AI in ERP, BI, and FP&A platforms, as well as generative tools for analysis and presentations. At the same time, the role remains strongly dependent on responsibility, judgment, internal control, and management dialogue, which means that AI is primarily used as decision support rather than as a replacement. In Sweden, the signals are therefore above the pilot level but not yet fully mainstream across the labor market, especially in smaller companies and the public sector. Overall, the role fits best in the range of 6-7.

Hit hör inte
Generaldirektör, myndighet (1112)
Verkställande direktör i företag utan chefshierarki (se respektive verksamhet)
descriptionEnglish: Plans, leads, and coordinates operations in companies and business enterprises with support from other managers, according to guidelines set by a board or management. Formulates and decides on goals and policy. Makes decisions regarding organizational structure and major investments. Represents the company in its dealings with other firms, organizations, and authorities. Reports results to the board or management.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Chief executives primarily engage in strategic decision-making, goal and policy formulation, budget/investment decisions, reporting, and coordination with the board and other managers. Much of this work is knowledge- and document-based and is therefore significantly exposed to AI support in analysis, compilation, decision-making support, environmental monitoring, and communication. At the same time, the role demands a high degree of responsibility, judgment, negotiation, leadership, and external representation, which suggests that AI is likely to complement rather than replace core tasks. Hence, it has a high but not maximal theoretical exposure to AI.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 1120 (executive directors and similar roles), there are clear but not yet fully mainstreamed AI signals in early 2026: executives are using generative AI tools for meeting materials, strategic documents, report analysis, scenario planning, and communication. This is also evident in leadership and executive coaching tools, as well as in broader corporate adoption where management uses AI for decision support and information compilation. At the same time, this is still more of a support in the workflow than a standardized, deeply integrated "core tool" for the role, and usage varies greatly between companies, industries, and levels of maturity. Therefore, for Swedish CEO roles, there are good current adoption signs, but not at the level of 6–7 where AI is established and widely operationally used.

description: Fattar beslut, leder och påverkar verksamheten i demokratiskt valda organ på nationell, regional eller lokal nivå. Utformar, stadfäster, ändrar och upphäver lagar, förordningar och andra författningar.
descriptionEnglish: Makes decisions, leads, and influences operations in democratically elected bodies at national, regional, or local levels. Formulates, ratifies, amends, and repeals laws, regulations, and other legal provisions.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Politicians’ core work is overwhelmingly cognitive and knowledge-based: reviewing information, debating policy, drafting or amending laws and regulations, making decisions, and communicating positions. These are tasks that AI can strongly assist with through summarization, scenario analysis, speech drafting, legislative comparison, and policy research. The role still requires human judgment, democratic legitimacy, negotiation, and public accountability, so AI is unlikely to replace the core office, but the task structure is highly exposed.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish politicians, there are observable AI-use signals, but they are still mostly limited to experimentation and adjacent support functions rather than routine, mainstream adoption in the core political role. In 2026, politicians and their staff do use generative AI for drafting speeches, summarising documents, preparing briefing notes, translating, and monitoring media/public sentiment, but these uses are uneven and often informal. Swedish parliament, ministries, and political parties have discussed AI governance and some offices have piloted tools, yet public-sector caution, confidentiality concerns, and the accountability nature of political decision-making keep adoption below broad operational use. Overall, this is best rated as marginal-to-early adoption rather than significant mainstream use.

nameEnglish: Chief Executive Officers at National and Local Government Levels
description: Organiserar, leder och svarar för att politiska beslut verkställs. Planerar, leder och samordnar myndigheters, kommuners och landstings verksamhet. Ger den politiska ledningen råd i sakfrågor.
descriptionEnglish: Organizes, leads, and ensures that political decisions are implemented. Plans, leads, and coordinates the activities of government agencies, local authorities, and county councils. Advises political leadership on substantive issues.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: This is a highly knowledge- and decision-intensive management role focused on planning, coordination, analysis, policy work, and providing advice to political leadership. A significant portion of the work consists of document review, preparation, strategic considerations, reports, and decision-making materials where AI can provide strong support. At the same time, the role requires a high degree of human judgment, responsibility, negotiation, internal/external coordination, and political legitimacy, which means it cannot be fully automated. Overall: high but not maximal AI exposure.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 1112 (general, county council, and municipal directors), there are clear current AI signals in Sweden, but they are mostly at the level of early experimentation and executive support rather than routine operational use. In the public sector, AI is increasingly discussed for decision support, document drafting, meeting summaries, policy analysis, and internal knowledge search, and top municipal/region executives are among the roles most likely to be exposed to these tools. However, there is limited evidence that AI is yet a mainstream, embedded part of the daily job across Swedish municipalities/regions/state authorities. Adoption is constrained by procurement, governance, legal/privacy requirements, and cautious public-sector implementation. So the best fit is early adoption with some real deployment, but not broad or mature usage.

description: Planerar, leder och samordnar verksamhet i intresseorganisationer. Svarar för förhandlingar, intressebevakning och information. Representerar organisationen och dess medlemmar i utredningar, uppvaktningar, remisser och dyl.
descriptionEnglish: Plans, leads, and coordinates activities in interest organizations. Responsible for negotiations, advocacy, and information sharing. Represents the organization and its members in inquiries, lobbying, consultations, and similar activities.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: No forecast available.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: No forecast available.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: The role is largely knowledge- and office-based: planning, management, coordination, negotiations, interest representation, responses to consultations, and representation entail a significant amount of text work, analysis, information gathering, and decision-making materials that AI can strongly support. At the same time, the assignment often requires relationship building, real-time negotiations, political judgment, and legitimacy towards members and counterparts, which limits full automation. In the Swedish context, strong collective agreements and organization-specific processes can hinder implementation, but the theoretical exposure remains high.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For SSYK 1113 (executive officials in interest organizations), there are clear current AI signals, but they are still mostly early-stage rather than deeply embedded. Observable use cases include AI-assisted drafting of letters, policy briefs, consultation responses, meeting summaries, media monitoring, member communications, and translation/summarization for stakeholder work. These tools are readily available and likely used by some unions, trade associations, employer organizations, and advocacy bodies in Sweden. However, compared with more standardized knowledge work, adoption is not yet mainstream or consistently documented across employers. The occupation depends heavily on negotiation, representation, relationship management, and judgment in politically sensitive contexts, which limits full automation and slows broad deployment. Overall, this fits early adoption: some employers are experimenting, and some teams use LLM-based tools, but current adoption is not yet at the level of routine, widespread operational dependence.

descriptionEnglish: Participates in the planning and execution of military operations, nationally and internationally. Trains and specializes in their service area within technical service, naval warfare, air warfare, ground warfare, command, or logistics. Leads lower military units.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Specialistofficerare perform a mixed role: planning and coordinating military operations, training personnel, and providing specialist expertise in technical, naval, air, land, command, or logistics functions. The planning, documentation, analysis, and training-preparation parts are fairly AI-exposable, but the job also involves leadership of lower military units, operational judgment, and work in real-world military environments where human authority, situational awareness, and physical presence matter. Overall this is a moderate, mixed exposure occupation rather than a mostly digital one.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish specialist officers, there are some observable AI-adoption signals, but they appear limited and mostly indirect. The occupation involves military planning, training, command, logistics, and technical specialization—areas where AI could support analysis, simulation, decision support, and administrative drafting. However, as of early 2026 there is little public evidence of broad operational deployment of generative AI or AI copilots in routine specialist-officer work. Defense organizations are typically cautious due to security, secrecy, interoperability, and procurement constraints, so adoption is likely confined to pilots, decision-support experiments, logistics optimization, and restricted internal tools rather than mainstream everyday use. Overall this looks like early adoption / pilot-stage rather than significant current deployment.

As of early 2026, this points to limited practical adoption in the occupation, with some experimentation but not broad, visible deployment.

nameEnglish: Care Assistants, Home Care, Nursing Home, and Rehabilitation
description:
Ger individuell vård och omsorg till äldre i eget eller särskilt boende. Utför hälso- och sjukvårdsuppgifter efter instruktion och delegering t.ex. kvalificerad sårvård och kateterspolning. Ger stöd, assistans, omvårdnad och social service till barn, äldre och personer med funktionsnedsättning i deras vardagsliv. Ger stimulans och stöd till individen i aktiviteter och kontakter med omvärlden.

Hit hör inte
Habiliteringsassistent (5330)
Hemvårdsassistent (3411)
descriptionEnglish: Provides individual care and support to the elderly in their own homes or in designated facilities. Executes healthcare tasks according to instructions and delegation, e.g., qualified wound care and catheter flushing. Supports, assists, cares for, and provides social services to children, the elderly, and people with disabilities in their daily lives. Stimulates and assists individuals in activities and interactions with the wider world.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: The Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that there will be very little competition for jobs for nursing assistants over the next year. This means that individuals with the required education or experience will have very large job opportunities, but the labor market may vary depending on the direction within the profession.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: In two years, the Swedish Public Employment Service assesses that job opportunities for nursing assistants will be very large. Job opportunities in the profession increase if you have relevant certificates, qualifications, or professional credentials.
forecast.ingress: Bedömningen för undersköterskor är en samlad bedömning för undersköterskor, vård- och specialavdelning och mottagning, undersköterskor, hemtjänst, hemsjukvård, äldreboende och habilitering, barnsköterskor. Det finns cirka 178300 yrkesverksamma undersköterskor i Sverige, varav cirka 91 procent är kvinnor och 9 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Nursing assistants within home care, home health care, nursing homes, and habilitation primarily work with practical care, support in daily life, and close personal contact. Some tasks are knowledge- and documentation-intensive, such as following instructions, reporting observations, and performing certain health care tasks after delegation, which provides some exposure to AI. However, the core of the work is physical, relationship-based, and highly situation-dependent, with many aspects requiring human presence and judgment in real time. Therefore, the exposure is low to medium and best placed around 4.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For care assistants in home care, home healthcare, elderly care, and habilitation, there are some observable AI adoption signals, but they remain limited and not yet mainstream in day-to-day work. The strongest current signals are indirect: Swedish municipalities and regional care providers increasingly use AI-enabled scheduling, documentation support, voice-to-text, decision support, and welfare-tech platforms, and there are pilots around fall detection, remote monitoring, and care-planning optimization. However, frontline care assistant work itself is still highly hands-on, regulated, and driven by staff shortages, so most AI is used in adjacent administrative or coordination tasks rather than in direct care delivery. Public-sector procurement and deployment are uneven, implementation is slow, and many workplaces still rely on conventional digital systems rather than embedded AI tools. Overall, this fits early adoption with some active experimentation and scattered deployments, but not broad, routine AI use across the occupation.

nameSwedish: Undersköterskor, vård- och specialavdelning och mottagning
nameEnglish: Care Assistants, Patient and Special Units and Clinics
description: Kontrollerar patienters kroppstemperatur, puls och blodtryck, tar prover och lägger om sår. Hjälper patienter med deras personliga hygien. Bäddar, delar ut mat och gör annat som ingår i den dagliga vården. Assisterar läkare och sjuksköterskor vid undersökningar och behandlingar. Tar prover, tar fram provsvar och journaler samt förbereder patienter för behandling. Plockar fram instrument inför undersökningar och ingrepp samt rengör och desinficerar dem.
descriptionEnglish: Monitors patients' body temperature, pulse, and blood pressure; collects samples; and manages wound dressings. Assists patients with personal hygiene. Makes beds, serves food, and performs other tasks included in daily care. Assists doctors and nurses during examinations and treatments. Collects samples, retrieves lab results and patient records, and prepares patients for treatments. Sets out instruments for examinations and procedures and cleans and disinfects them.

forecast.shortTermOutlook: Arbetsförmedlingen bedömer att det kommer vara mycket liten konkurrens om jobben för undersköterskor under det närmaste året. Det innebär att personer som har den utbildning eller erfarenhet som krävs kommer ha mycket stora möjligheter till arbete, men beroende på inriktning inom yrket kan arbetsmarknaden variera.
forecast.mediumTermOutlook: På 2 års sikt bedömer Arbetsförmedlingen att möjligheterna till arbete för undersköterskor kommer vara mycket stora. Möjligheterna till arbete inom yrket ökar om du har relevanta certifikat, behörigheter eller yrkesbevis.
forecast.ingress: Bedömningen för undersköterskor är en samlad bedömning för undersköterskor, vård- och specialavdelning och mottagning, undersköterskor, hemtjänst, hemsjukvård, äldreboende och habilitering, barnsköterskor. Det finns cirka 178300 yrkesverksamma undersköterskor i Sverige, varav cirka 91 procent är kvinnor och 9 procent är män.

scores.theoreticalExposureRationale: Nursing assistants in healthcare and specialized departments/clinics work in a clearly mixed environment with both practical and administrative elements. Some tasks are based on information and routines, such as retrieving test results and medical records, documenting, and preparing patients, which AI can support. However, the core of the work consists of close patient contact, hands-on care, sample collection, wound care, hygiene assistance, preparations for examinations, and cleaning and disinfection of equipment, which requires physical presence, judgment, and human interaction. In the Swedish healthcare context, regulations and work organization also limit how much can be automated. Overall, the exposure is moderate and somewhat lower than for more administratively focused healthcare work.
scores.currentAdoptionRationale: For Swedish nursing assistants in care and special departments/clinics, there are some observable AI signals, but they remain marginal in day-to-day practice as of early 2026. AI is more clearly present in adjacent clinical administration and decision-support areas than in the core hands-on tasks of this occupation. In Sweden, hospitals and regions are testing AI for triage support, documentation, scheduling, symptom chat, and image/scan analysis, but nursing assistants typically are not the primary end users of these tools. Their work is still dominated by physical care, vital-sign checks, hygiene, sample handling, patient assistance, and equipment preparation, which have limited direct AI penetration. There may be localized pilots for speech-to-text documentation, automated patient flow, or digital monitoring, but these are not yet mainstream across employers. Overall, this fits early experimentation rather than broad adoption.
